title: gh#9: Perf: Cache /api/settings/storage aggregate query
reasoning:
Imported from https://github.com/joshgk00/social-media-scheduler/issues/9.
Backlog section: Performance.

## Problem

`GET /api/settings/storage` runs an unbounded SUM/COUNT aggregate across the entire post_media table on every request. No cache, no user scoping.

## Identified by

Code quality review Run 1 — performance-optimizer (perf-004)

## Fix

Add a 5-minute Redis cache keyed by userId. Also scope the query to the authenticated user's media.

## Files

- `packages/api/src/routes/settings.ts:285-307`
reproduction: `GET /api/settings/storage` runs an unbounded SUM/COUNT aggregate across the entire post_media table on every request. No cache, no user scoping.
recommendation: Add a 5-minute Redis cache keyed by userId. Also scope the query to the authenticated user's media.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: Imported from GitHub issue. Preserve or add focused regression coverage when the issue acceptance criteria calls for it.

history.reasoning: The original evidence path still exists. The storage endpoint has moved to packages/api/src/routes/settings.ts:303-323, but it still executes a direct aggregate over FROM post_media with only WHERE deleted_at IS NULL. There is no user_id/userId predicate and no Redis get/set/cache use in that handler; redis is only used by the sessions route. packages/db/src/schema/post-media.ts confirms post_media has a user_id column and index, so user scoping remains applicable. Current route tests in packages/api/src/__tests__/routes/settings.test.ts only assert 401 and response shape/value mapping for mocked db.execute results; they do not assert user scoping or caching, and the mock Redis only exposes scanStream. I attempted focused Vitest runs, but they could not execute in this read-only sandbox because Vite/Vitest tried to create temp/cache directories. The current code evidence is sufficient that the finding remains open.
commands: pnpm --filter @sms/api exec vitest run src/__tests__/routes/settings.test.ts --configLoader runner

history.reasoning: The original evidence path still exists. The storage handler is now at packages/api/src/routes/settings.ts:304-334 and no longer performs the original unscoped uncached aggregate: it defines a 5-minute TTL constant, reads Redis key settings:storage:${userId}, returns cached JSON when present, scopes the aggregate with WHERE user_id = ${req.session.userId!} AND deleted_at IS NULL, then writes the computed usage back to Redis with EX 300. The post_media schema still has user_id and a user index, so the scope is meaningful. Current tests in packages/api/src/__tests__/routes/settings.test.ts now assert the Redis get/set key and TTL, the user-scoped SQL predicate, and the cached path avoiding db.execute. I attempted focused Vitest runs, but this read-only sandbox prevents Vite/Vitest from creating temp/cache directories, so tests could not execute here. Current code and test evidence indicate the finding is fixed.
commands: pnpm --filter @sms/api exec vitest run src/__tests__/routes/settings.test.ts --configLoader runner
rg -n "STORAGE_USAGE_CACHE_TTL_SECONDS|settings:storage|redis\.get|redis\.set|FROM post_media|WHERE user_id|deleted_at IS NULL" packages/api/src/routes/settings.ts -S

history.reasoning: The original evidence path still exists, but the storage handler has moved to packages/api/src/routes/settings.ts:304-352 and now implements the requested fix. It uses a 5-minute TTL constant, reads Redis key settings:storage:${userId}, returns valid cached JSON without querying the database, scopes the aggregate with WHERE user_id = ${userId} AND deleted_at IS NULL, then writes the computed usage back to Redis with EX 300. The post_media schema still has user_id and a user_id index, so the scoping is meaningful. Current tests in packages/api/src/__tests__/routes/settings.test.ts assert Redis get/set behavior, cache-hit database bypass, the user-scoped SQL predicate, and fallback behavior for Redis read/malformed-cache cases. Focused Vitest execution could not run in this read-only sandbox because Vitest attempted to create a temp ssr directory and failed with EPERM, but pnpm --filter @sms/api typecheck completed successfully. Current code and test evidence indicate the finding is fixed.
commands: rg -n "settings/storage|STORAGE_USAGE_CACHE_TTL_SECONDS|settings:storage|redis\.(get|set)|FROM post_media|WHERE user_id|deleted_at IS NULL|post_media" packages/api/src/routes/settings.ts packages/api/src/__tests__/routes/settings.test.ts packages/db/src/schema/post-media.ts -S
pnpm --filter @sms/api exec vitest run src/__tests__/routes/settings.test.ts --configLoader runner
